title: Speed of light
short: The speed of electromagnetic waves in vacuum, c ≈ 3.00 × 10⁸ m/s — nature's universal speed limit.
full:
The speed of light in vacuum, c ≈ 299 792 458 m/s (about 3 × 10⁸ m/s), is the same for every observer and is the fastest anything carrying information can travel. All electromagnetic waves — radio, light, X-rays — move at c in vacuum.

For light it links wavelength and frequency, c = λ·f, so a known frequency fixes the wavelength. Light slows down in glass or water, which is what makes it bend (refraction).

The SI unit is metres per second (m/s).
